AUGUST UPDATE @Partners We have been working hard to release large monthly updates this year and since we went into lockdown these have become a little more challenging to deliver. We also have started to focus heavily on live events in and out of the game including Twitch Drops, Monthly/Weekly Events, Daily Bounties and Custom Servers. As we move through the next 12 months you will see us start to refine these as they become a big focus for how we sustain engagement in SoT and deliver new opportunities for players and creators. In order for us to deliver at the size and quality we expect for a monthly update in August we will not release a Content Update to Sea of Thieves, all planned feature work will now release in September. The Pirate Emporium will also not receive any new content or functionality. We will be communicating to players that our next Content Update will now launch in September with Vaults of the Ancients . This is however still very much under NDA until we do. We are planning on releasing a minor update to retail with our regular bug fixes and support for the pre-planned August Live Events to keep players engaged until Vaults of the Ancients lands in September. This is planned to land on August 19th What does this mean for future updates? This in no way indicates a change to our regular monthly cadence. We are confident in our strategy that monthly releases is the right cadence for maintaining engagement. If you have any questions please do not hesitate to contact me via DM. Thanks again for all your support (edited)

Hey @Partners ! It has been a little while and I have been squirreled away in the back doing some work on making the bug reporting and visibility situation a little better for you all. So here I am with an update on where we currently are, along with some information on exactly HOW we fix bugs, which should hopefully give you all a bit of context on why some bugs may get fixed and others are a bit more tricky!
Hope you are all prepared for some walls of text

So without further ado, let’s get into it! Firstly, the current plan is that We would like to trial using a Trello board specifically within the Partner Program. We have a robust bug tracking system on our end, but unfortunately there is no way for us to give you any visibility on that due to a myriad of reasons, especially those involving data privacy. This board will be our way of giving you more visibility into our bug fixing process. We understand that it can be frustrating when it feels like your bug reports are being thrown into the wind without any real acknowledgement that they have even been received let alone actively worked on. The hope here is that when a bug is reported by you all, we will be able to give some form of visibility on where we are with reproducing or fixing that bug, which should help you feel better knowing we are seeing and working on these bugs as we get them.

But what exactly is needed for us to fix a bug? This Is where I want to outline how we go about fixing bugs, to give you all an idea of what you can do to help the process along as best it can. When we receive a bug report, the most important factor in that bug being addressed is our ability to consistently replicate the bug’s occurrence. This is for a multitude of reasons, not the least of which being the fact that it allows us to actually understand what it causing the bug, and what we might be able to try to fix it. It then allows us to test that fix reliably as we know we can reproduce the bug well enough to see if the fix worked. To this end, the most important thing you can give us when reporting a bug is specific context and reproduction steps . If you have managed to clip the bug in action, amazing. Even better if you can reproduce it yourself. This gives us a lot of information that you might not think was important but can often be the difference between consistent reproduction and a complete inability to reproduce at all. The most useful thing you could give us is a link to the stream it happened in with a timestamp of roughly when it happened , as this provides us with the context of what was happening in the game world in the leadup to the bug happening, which could be essential for reproduction. If you don’t have a video clip of the bug, you can still make the report! However: The most vital thing if you are only providing a written report of a bug is a concrete rundown of exactly what you were doing and where in a step-by-step process. Where were you standing on your ship? What area of the map were you in? Were there any world events going on at the time? Were any crewmates or enemies dead on your ship at the time? These are all things that may not immediately be obvious causes of a problem but can often be the one thing we are missing to reproduce a particularly nasty bug, so as much detail as possible is key.

Even with all this information, sometimes a bug may be inherently tied to a core system of the game, and even with the ability to reproduce it consistently, if the bug happens to be due to interactions between core systems of the game, this could require months of dedicated work to try and resolve, so sometimes being able to replicate it doesn’t mean the fix is by any means easy to implement. However, we will always endeavour to try and fix as many bugs as we can to the best of our ability.

Once we have this info, what’s next? When this is all set up, we will be doing daily sweeps of the bug reporting channel. Once we spot your bug report in this channel, we will use this reaction ( ) to acknowledge that we have seen it. We will then send the report off to our customer support team, who will help join the dots if this is an existing bug or create a new ticket if it’s a fresh one. Once we have this information back, we will update and add to the Trello board. We will then delete your bug report from the reporting channel. This is to keep things visibly clear which bugs have been sent over and updated on the Trello to avoid double reports. However, we will of course still have all the info you sent us, and the necessary details will appear in the Trello.

Hopefully this gives you all an idea of where we are right now on making Bug reporting clearer and more efficient! Keep an eye out in coming weeks for when the Trello board hopefully appears, as it will be your Go-to place to check if other partners are experiencing similar bugs, and to help get your feedback assigned to the right place.
Cheers!

Hey @Partners - I have been looking at ways to help out with the Streamsniping issues that some of you have by changing your Gamertag on your main account, while keeping it safe so that no one else can take it - so if any of you are interested in this, let me know. Basically, you would: 1) Create an alt Xbox account name with your streamer Gamertag in it, something like KaidaWrathPP, PhuzzyPP, etc is fine 2) DM me the alt Gamertag along with the Gamertag you currently use 3) It will then take a day or two and the alt account name would be switched onto your main account, and your Streamer name is then safely parked on the alt so that no one else can take it After the switch takes place, I can then freely give you Gamertag name changes as and when you need them. If you ever leave the partner program, you can either keep everything as it is or, request your Streamer name be moved back to your main account again. Please bear in mind though, that the parked Gamertag on the alt account cannot be moved around easily by us, it’s a very manual process for the Xbox platform team to modify accounts in this way, and it is not something that would normally even be considered, this is purely a favor to Rare to help out in this way. If this is something you are interested in doing, just DM me your main account name and the alt you created, and I will get them set up next week. (edited)

Hey @Partners ! With this being the last working day of the year here at Rare, and what a year it has been, I wanted to take some time to talk about where we have been, where we are and where we are going. 2020 has been hard. I am personally very grateful that I continue to have a job and that I am in good health when so many have been impacted. I know this can be hard on individuals and families and I want you all to know that we are here to support where we can. I never want the Partner Programme to feel like a pressure for any of you and if you need time or space please let me know. But there has been a lot to celebrate! 2020 has been the biggest year for Sea of Thieves to date and that in no small part has been accelerated by all you amazing people. Our numbers have been up across everything and the launch of Steam has seen us really expand our PC audience and diversify our player base from countries across the globe who may not have previously been able to access the game. This in turn means that our viewing numbers have been on the rise and we have more content creators from more countries experiencing the game for the first time and bringing in fresh new faces to the seas.

This game continues to be a project of passion for the team here at Rare and I want to thank you all for your support as we continue to navigate these uncharted waters and for celebrating the highs with us and for having our backs during the lows. We know we have work to do and your feedback is super valuable. We expect 2021 to be even bigger than this year. Seasons will be our new foundation for delivering content to the game and will create a sustainable way for us to keep players engaged. Custom Servers and our plans to give you access to the toybox will push forward in a big way next year and I can’t wait to see what you create and what new types of content start popping up. We are putting more investment into Sea of Thieves than we ever have and there will be some time at the start of the year where we are going to have to ask you to trust us and that we promise what is coming will be worth the wait.

For the Partner Programme itself we are going to continue to see things change and grow we will have new processes put in places for bugs and feedback in early 2021 and we are going to see more new partners join like we have over the past few weeks. We also have big plans for Sea of Thieves on Twitch with more places to highlight Partners and the creator community, the addition of Twitch Drops being turned on 24/7 with an RNG element and the Partner Directory on the seaofthieves.com site. All in all I think we have a lot to look forward to in 2021 and I hope that we continue to see the amazing content we have come to expect from your channels and also the incredible support you have given each other this year. Its humbling to see so many of you give up your time to share experiences or just lend an ear to each other. You are all amazing! Lastly I will be here checking in over the holidays so I am not completely gone so if you have any issues please do reach out. Keep up the great work Crew have a great holiday period and I will see you on the seas.

Hey @everyone ! The time has come, and we are finally ready to introduce you all to your new bug tracking Trello board! Below you’ll find two links, one to the Trello board and the other to a video explanation of how the process works if you want a TL:DR of the system, although it is fairly simple:

New bug reports made by yourselves are posted in the ‘bugs here please’ Discord text channel. To acknowledge I have seen it, I will respond with a and then send the bugs you’ve reported that day off to our Customer Support team, to have an official report logged in our system. Once the official report has been assigned a ticket number [those digits at the start of each bug on the board] I will add your report to the Trello board. The sections are as follows: About section contains an FAQ of most common questions and a guide to the tags marking each report. Reported issues houses every report made that isn’t awaiting a fix or has already been fixed. The status of each bug is denoted by its tags. Pending fixes is for any bugs awaiting fix implementations in the next retail release. Recent fixes contains all bugs that have had fixes implemented in the latest retail release. Fix archive is used for any bugs that have been fixed in previous patches. Patch notes will be home to an archive of the patch notes for each release. End of each day I will be wiping the ‘bugs here please’ chat for easier visibility. (edited)

Have a poke around and feel free to drop me a message if you have any questions not covered in the FAQ! I would recommend watching the video for a slightly more in-depth explanation of how things work. I’d also like to reiterate that this board is strictly under NDA and only for Partners, so please do not share these links anywhere outside of the Partner Programme Discord Server, and do not show them on-stream. https://microsofteur-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/jomcmurt_microsoft_com/Er3oLhAWG2FBte6JdzOXuGQBUgwAYr9hP6081JdSUKBvQQ?e=pF2Ypp https://trello.com/b/ITV6X7F4/artemis-tracker

@Partners I have seen some confusion over terminology with Season 1 which is completely understandable given how close it is to other games systems but with a twist so thought I would put this glossary up for you to have a look it: Season: Introduced by Content Update Lasts ~3 months New content, rewards, progression goals and live events over the period. Pirate Log: New Progression System Renown and Deeds 100 rewards to earn Completely free to all players Plunder Pass: Paid add on Get 11 additional Emporium items Heavily discounted rate but need to be unlocked through play Available the following Season in the Emporium to purchase individually Noticing some folks referring to the new progression system as the Plunder Pass but that is the paid bundle. Reach out if you have any questions.

Hey @everyone , As many of you are highly invested Sea of Thieves players, I am sure many of you have been on the latest Insider build and have come across the new Glitterbeard Easter egg that we placed in the game. We created this Easter egg to honour a good friend to many and great developer at Rare who is unfortunately no longer with us, and it's something that we really wish to be something both special and beautiful for the players to discover on their own when it finally makes its way into the live build. As such, we think a lot of the Joy of this Easter egg lies in its discovery, and that perhaps detailed guides and walkthroughs might detract from the experience. So just this once we were hoping that we could try and leave some of the magic for the players, and lean away from detailed Wiki documentation or how-to's. Of course, you are welcome to enjoy this Easter egg together with your communities, and we hope they love it as much as we do.

Hello @Partners ! Just a quick one from me. We've recently been tightening up the Official Sea of Thieves Discord ahead of all the exciting things we are going to be doing as we continue to grow the game. We've seen tremendous growth across all ages in our community, so ensuring the Discord is family friendly is a high priority for us. As such, we've had to crack down on the TOS for the server, and one of these rules involves Discord's "Status message" feature. Right now, it is against our TOS to use this status system for any form of self promotion (excluding streaming mode), and to display any vulgarity, racism, toxicity etc. We understand the benefit of using self-promotion statuses in other Discord servers. So we feel that it might perhaps be easier if you wish to have more freedom around using Statuses, to leave the Official Discord to keep everything above board. If you wish to remain inside the Official Discord however, we would like to ask that you make sure your Status messages are compliant with these terms. This includes attempts to bypass the bot filters for vulgarity or links (such as spelling out D I C C) Please be aware that from Monday, moderators will begin tidying this space, so you have this weekend to make any changes or leave the server if you wish before they do the cleanup. If you receive any notifications of muting or being warned, it will most likely be related to this! Thanks a lot! (edited)

MONDAY MORNING UPDATE Hey @everyone ! I thought I might start posting on a Monday morning with some updates on what's going on to keep everyone up to date. We have a tonne of stuff happening at the moment so I thought I would try and get it all in one place. Firstly, I would like to welcome aboard the 19 new Partners who recently joined the programme from around the world taking us to a grand total of 109 Sea of Thieves Partners . Combined you had 4.1 million watch hours of Sea of Thieves on Twitch in the last 30 days! • This week we have the final update for Season 1 which brings with it: o more items to the Pirate Emporium (Sea of Sands Set) o more Dark Adventurer items for Legends o more items to Outposts (Venemous Kraken Set, Distinguished Admiral Set and the Legacy Event Cosmetics from Festival of Fishing) o and a bunch of bugs fixes and QoL improvements o Vault Raiders Live Event from March 25th See below for an early look at the WIP patch notes. Hey @Partners , I wanted to take some time to go through what I feel is a growing concern for me in the Discord server: the tone and lack of professional etiquette from some Partners. Before I get into this, I do want to say that thankfully these occurrences are few and far between for the moment, but also want to make it clear that this type of emotive, unprofessional dialogue is not welcome within the Discord. I understand that folks who are in here have a huge amount of passion for the game and that passion can manifest itself as aggressive venting within the channels here. This, however, is not the place to vent. We don’t shy away from reading constructive feedback, bug reports, interesting new ideas or requests to change features or processes. In fact we often look to you as a channel who speak not just for yourselves, but your communities - and by extension, ours - too. I look at even just the recent investigation into ship respawn distance as a positive change brought forward by our ongoing dialogue with you, and we're making further steps to formalise your feedback track into the studio and ensure your voices are heard.

However, more and more I am seeing “I hate…”, “This is fu***ing annoying” or “It’s just lazy…” and it’s not constructive or insightful to read - it's painful and often incredibly reductive. It’s not motivating or welcoming for other Partners who strive to create a positive social environment and wish to share their feedback with balance and nuance, and I know it can be particularly hard to read for the dev team, who pour their heart into everything we try and do. With that said: from here on in, we will be creating a new role within the Discord. If we feel that people continually fail to meet the level of professional etiquette we strive to achieve in the server, this “read only” role will be applied. If those with that role continue to direct nonconstructive and negative comments towards us over other channels, we may even revisit their Partnered status. Feedback isn't always going to be glowing - and that we fully accept - but it must always be respectful, and we won't tolerate that line being crossed multiple times. We want the Discord to be a positive social space everyone feels like they can speak up, share ideas and collaborate. A place where everyone feels excited for the possibility of what Sea of Thieves and the Partner Programme can bring to your communities and businesses.

Hey @Partners , As you know DMCA can be a tricky one to navigate and we have been working with music and platform partners and legal teams to craft a message everyone feels comfortable with. The below is our statement on the matter which should not be posted outside of the Discord. As it's very much a legal matter I can't comment any more than than what we have here but hope this give you an idea of what to expect. "Sea of Thieves: A Pirate’s Life contains elements of licensed music throughout the five Tall Tales which make up the original story delivered as part of the update. We are working with our music and platform partners to avoid any critical issues for creating content for Sea of Thieves: A Pirate’s Life. However, content, especially videos on demand, may be subject to monetisation claims by third-party rights owners. Please check the terms of your platform. We are currently working on a future technical update to provide a toggle that will allow creators to replace the licensed music with our original music. If you encounter any issues creating content for Sea of Thieves: A Pirate’s Life please contact us at creators@seaofthieves.com" The obvious question I am sure you will all ask is "Are we likely to get strikes or takedowns?" the response here is: "To the best of our knowledge, we don’t believe strikes or take downs are the intent. Morning @Partners , I wanted to highlight the known issues of the Patch Notes for you as there are some workarounds here. Especially for those of you planning to blast through all the content today. Known Issues ‘A Pirate’s Life’ Tall Tale – Joining in Progress Joining a crew who have already made progress through checkpoints in the first Tale will spawn players in the incorrect location. For the lost player, dying and returning will bring them to the correct location in the Tale. ‘Lords of the Sea’ Tall Tale – Visual Issues Crews who have recently returned from the Tunnels of the Damned and then begin their journey through the ‘Lords of the Sea’ Tall Tale in the same gameplay session will encounter visual issues. As a workaround, this can be fixed by forcing the game to quit by using Alt+F4 on PC or closing the application on console and then rejoining your session. (edited)

A Pirate’s Life’ Tall Tale – Joining in Progress Tunnels of the Damned – Rejoining a Session While travelling through the Tunnels of the Damned, losing your connection to the game and rejoining may cause visual issues and cause the migration to the new area to fail, returning you to your starting location. New Tall Tale Commendations – Tale Visibility Players are able to browse Commendations for ‘The Sunken Pearl’ and ‘Dark Brethren’ Tall Tales before they have completed the preceding Tale. To prevent the experience from being spoiled, avoid reading ahead! Ranged and Melee Weapon Hit Detection In areas of intense action, players may find themselves firing shots or landing strikes that do not cause damage to their targets. While improvements continue to be delivered during our content updates, this remains a key priority for the team. To learn more about known issues in Sea of Thieves currently being tracked and their status, head over to our Known Issues support site article. (edited)

Hey @Partners ! As you may have seen we have soft launched Sea of Thieves Television on the Sea of Thieves Twitch Channel. After taking the Developer Stream off the air we wanted to see what we could do to make the channel more inclusive and a place that truly showcases the breadth and talent within our community. We thought why the hell not just go all in! So that's what we have done. We want Sea of Thieves Television to grow and evolve with the community much like the game has with regular programming like: Partner Showcase (A 2 hour stream with one of our amazing Partners) The Gallery (Whether it's Art, Cosplay, Props or Music this is the place to come watch our community get creative) Maiden Voyage (New to Sea of Thieves? come watch these Creators as they guide you on you adventures) Gold Rush (How much gold can you cash in within your 2 hour limit? Climb your way up those leader-boards) Premier Events (Starting with the Plunder Games we always want to make sure you have a premier event to follow along with, more exciting things in the works) and a bunch more series currently in production

We want Sea of Thieves Television to become a destination for viewers to find Creators and community members they might have missed as well as a place to come to be entertained by the best in class Partners we have here in the programme. Joe @Joe Simon @StatusHidden Liam @lirolake and Heath @Edge are all here to make sure that the production of this runs smoothly they will be working directly with Rare to run the channel and I am sure will be reaching out to you all in time asking if you would like to participate. Thanks to all who have participated so far and thanks in advance for all your help on future shows. As always there will be bumps in the road as we get this up and running and processes in place but I am excited for the future of what we have here and in combination with this amazing community and custom servers I'm excited for the amazing content we will see on our screens! Hey @Partners , Tomorrow's Hotfix ( https://twitter.com/SeaOfThieves/status/1430151023587467266) will address a number of issues identified in our recent August update, however with the primary goal of improving the Alt+Tab experience on Steam. We've seen the reports that from the stalls a number of exploit cases have been identified allowing players to gain an unfair advantage over other crews. While we will have resolved the Alt+Tab issue, the stall created when enabling 'Replace Gamertags' will still be present in this hotfix. Therefore in tomorrow's Hotfix we will also disable the ability to bind 'Replace Gamertags' to a Key in order to mitigate the spread of these exploits further. Note - This will not affect players who have already bound this to a key, therefore should be seen as a mitigation for these issues gaining traction and growing wider and won't take this functionality away from anyone currently using it. Given the primary users of this feature is our creators i wanted to share this in advance and give some context as to why we've made this decision to protect players from these issues. Our next steps are to investigate the stall from the Replace Gamertags tool and work to return this functionality to players, however we will more importantly focus investigating the root cause of these exploits and work to address the core issue to prevent further issues being identified.

Morning @Partners ! I've recently been doing some investigating as a number of you have mentioned that the directory seemed to not quite be working for you, such as the directory displaying you as offline when you are streaming! After some investigation with the web team, it seems that the root cause of this is when you change your Twitch name. The way our directory works is it generates a twitch URL based on your name when you were added to the Directory , so for example: Name given in directory form: example1 Twitch URL generated: https://twitch.tv/example1 The way our directory checks if you are online is through checking this url , so if you change your username, your twitch URL also changes, but not on our end unless we know you've changed usernames! Afternoon @Partners ! I wanted to take some time to address the current plans for an April Fool’s Day wheelbarrow prank that is currently in motion. We as much as anyone understand the fun of jokes and lighthearted moments within the community. However, it's important to understand your position as Partners here, and the potential impact of actions like this. As Partners, you are all privy to information that is considered confidential, and as such there is a level of trust and credibility attached to the things you say, when you say them. When these kinds of jokes are spread over a long period of time they can undermine that trust, losing their intended effect as a joke and instead having unintended consequences that negatively impact the community. We purposefully do not do April Fool’s Day for Sea of Thieves or as Rare, because we’re all too aware of the potential repercussions. A prank on this scale could create a level of expectation and excitement around the proposed ‘feature’ that will only lead to eventual disappointment within the community. And this may, in turn, create unnecessary pressure on the studio team to create a feature that was never intended or planned for, which will only lead to wider disappointment when this feature is not delivered. While we understand there are no bad intentions here, we think it would be best to retire this idea before it picks up too much momentum, as the potential damage it could do if it gains traction is quite significant. Apologies that this likely isn't the thing you all want to hear, but we hope you understand that we would like to prevent any unnecessary disappointments or difficult conversations arising where they could easily be avoided.

Morning @Partners ! We are looking to test out some new drops systems based on Programme feedback, and we wanted to see which of the two current ideas you would like to try. These ideas have been formed to try and reduce the need for long 6 day drops periods that fatigue you and our players, as well as rotate through sets a bit more quickly so that we aren't giving out the same cosmetic set for a year! The ideas are as follows: IDEA 1: DOUBLE DROPS INCREASED TIME This idea basically means that on each individual day of say a 4 day period (the usual Friday to Monday period we are used to) there will be not 1 but 2 items available for drops per day. This means that in total, viewers will gain 8 drops items instead of 4 in total without having to watch 8 days worth of drops. This would be accompanied with an increase in the required viewing time for Drops, from 20 minutes to around 1-2hrs, but players will still be required to watch every day to earn each day's individual drops. IDEA 2: CUMULATIVE DROPS This idea would basically see players watching for a larger cumulative total across a drops period (say 4 or 5 hours for example) but upon achieving the watch time you unlock every drops item in that particular drop period. So for example if a viewer watches 5 hours of a streamer on friday, each hour they will unlock a new drop item from the available pool in the Friday-Monday period. This means the viewer can spread their watch time however they like across the weekend, but each hour is rewarded. If you could all vote either the Orange heart for IDEA 1 or the Purple heart for IDEA 2 we will take a look at the results at the end of this week and see how we would like to proceed, if you have any comments on these new systems, please feel free to drop them in the temporary channel we've spun up called #drops-feedback . (edited)

@everyone PLEASE READ As part of streamlining how we can help you and answer questions, and to allow us some downtime when we're off and away from DMs, we have introduced ModMail to the server. Any submissions via ModMail will ping both the Admiral and First Mate and someone will get back to you during their working hours. While we understand things need answering, and there are many timezones to cater to, this is to avoid an 'always on' feeling for those of us who are here for you - it will also mean that you get an answer as soon as someone is around whereas this isn't guaranteed if it is outside of working hours or if someone is on holiday etc. ModMail will also track who has answered what and what has been said, allowing us to have eyes on more of the conversations taking place here so we can best look after you all. Shorky and ZiNc have set up the new ModMail categories with instructions on how to use it and also were you can drop your queries. Just to be clear - YOUR QUERIES CAN NOT BE SEEN BY OTHERS! Once you open a ticket it will move to DMs so everything you ask or say is still private as we understand that is often why you reach out via DMs and not in the ask-rare channels etc. We still very much want to protect this privacy and allow for open and honest conversation away from the main channels, but also allow the team to share the load a bit. #modmail-info has the info you need but you can submit a question in any channel using the commands and it will remain private to you. (edited)

@Partners , @Partner Legends , @Affiliate Hi all, Ahead of the first Tall Tale of our Sea of Thieves: Legend to Monkey Island experience arriving tomorrow, we wanted to ensure you all knew where you stood in terms of licensed music content. As part of the launch of this Tale, we will be changing the front end menu music to a copyrighted tune - and as such, will potentially be something that could cause for strike action to be taken against creators. To keep yourselves as safe as possible, we suggest turning the 'Mute Copyright Audio' setting on, in the Game Settings menu, before the launch of the build tomorrow. This will be the best way to protect your channels against any action from that very first stream. Iconic music from the Monkey Island series will also be playing throughout the course this set of Tall Tales, so we recommend you keep this setting on when engaging with this content too. P.S. We have tested a let's play of the first Tall Tale on YouTube few days ago and it didn't raise any flags on upload, BUT that's not a 100% guarantee, especially in regards to that tune in the starting menu, so please be cautious around it tomorrow. Hopefully, you won't have any trouble at all!

@Partners , @Partner Legends , @Affiliate We've planned something very different for our next Community Weekend on September 30th – October 2nd and wanted to give you heads up ahead of public announcement! Please do not share anything below anywhere yet: Living the stream Item one on the agenda is how to increase the Community Emissary Grade, which takes on a brand new form for this Season’s Community Weekend fun. Whereas previously players could contribute to boosting the Grade level by using the #SeaOfThievesCommunityDay tag on Twitter, this time the level of multiplier – and more – will be powered by how much time players spend watching the Sea of Thieves category on Twitch! That’s right: all you need to do to help raise the Community Emissary Grade is tune into any of your favourite content creators in the Sea of Thieves category between 10am UTC on Saturday, 30th September and 10am UTC on Monday, 2nd October. As with previous Community events, as the Community Emissary Grade raises, so will the amount of gold, reputation, Renown and Allegiance available – with the top Grade offering two-and-a-half times the reward for your work! For those of you who are still yet to hit level 100 Renown for Season Nine, this will be your chance to race through those numbers and make a pretty penny at the same time. And if you’re a few levels off scoring your first Hourglass-related curse, the same applies to you too – this weekend serves as the perfect opportunity for you to maximise your progress and earn some serious stonks. Raising the Bar Talking of streams, the Rare crew will be setting sail on the Saturday morning, live from the on-site tavern! Tankards will be raised, chaos will ensue, pirates will plunder and a gay old time will be had. Join the gang from 10am UTC on Saturday, 30th September to kick off the event with a healthy dose of shenanigans. Every minute you spend watching it will also work towards raising that Community Emissary Grade, and may even earn you some Twitch Drops at the same time. That’s very efficient viewing, by our standards. Top of the Drops Tuning into your favourite Sea of Thieves content creators (and of course us, on the Saturday morning) will also work towards unlocking a sweet suite of Drops over the course of the weekend! As the Community Emissary Grade rises through the ranks, so does the amount of Drops available over the event. Things will kick off without any Drops on, so players will need to kickstart the programme of earnables by hitting the second Community Emissary Grade. Then, from Grade Two through to Grade Five, the Eastern Winds Ruby Sturdy Boots, Gilded Phoenix Cutlass, Eastern Winds Ruby Bejewelled Gloves and Gilded Phoenix Eye of Reach will all unlock Grade by Grade, and by the end of the event will – hopefully – all be available to swipe by watching your favourite streamer in the Sea of Thieves category for an hour per item. We’ve also set you a sneaky extra goal, which will bag you a bonus Drop should you rise to the challenge: if you all manage to get Sea of Thieves into the Top 10 categories (by highest viewers) on Twitch at any point in the weekend, you’ll unlock a very special Twitch Drop: the Obsidian Ship’s Crest. This is the first ever time we’ll have introduced the Obsidian Ship’s Crest to the Sea of Thieves, which will be exclusive to Sea of Thieves Community Weekends for the foreseeable future. This makes the Obsidian Ship’s Crest a super swish, super rare item to have in your inventory, which can only be equipped to Captained ships – so we suggest doing everything you can to get this outrageously cool item available for everyone over the weekend. Just make sure that your Sea of Thieves and Twitch accounts are linked ahead of time to ensure you benefit from all these brilliant bonus items on offer!

Presenting: Pop-Up Plunder It’s not just the increased rewards and more Twitch Drops you stand to benefit from as you race through the different Community Emissary Grades. Every time the community collectively levels up, we’ll introduce a new piece of Pop-Up Plunder to every single server of Sea of Thieves. But what is Pop-Up Plunder, we hear you cry? Well, we can answer that for you right now: it’s a way for us to sneak terrific treasures into the world on the fly. And if you think that sounds cool, it’s because it is. Every time Pop-Up Plunder becomes available, players will receive a notification in-game that fresh loot is live and that a clue regarding the whereabouts of the high-value haul has been released somewhere to Sea of Thieves’ official social media channels. There will only be one plunderous prize per server, and each will only be discoverable for an hour before it disappears – so you’ll have to be swift on social and on sea to secure your stash. However it’s not all the dreamy pirate’s life, as there’s a certain amount of strife involved in the retrieval of these special stashes of loot. Once the Pop-Up Plunder in question has been dug up, a cheeky little map marker will appear for everyone else sailing on the same sea – just so they know the precious prize hasn’t been handed in yet and they can still steal it. If you’re thinking, ‘well, that sounds dastardly’ – you’re right. We designed it like that, because we are dastardly. You’re welcome. Of course, if we’re suggesting that you might need to scrap to hold onto these spoils, it’s because we know they’re worth fighting for. Amongst the assortment of items that will be buried at random are packed Collector’s Chests, Chests of Legends, suites of Siren Gems and even Chest of Fortunes – so this is a spicy game of loot roulette that stands to swipe you scores of cold hard gold. Finally: even after the community have hit Grade Five, we’ll continue to periodically drop Pop-Up Plunder into the world until the end of the event – just because we can. And, while the vast majority of the loot drops will be at random, the only guaranteed Pop-Up Plunder item will be Grade Five’s treasure, which you’ll all recognise as an item particularly rare and Wondrous. Don’t expect that particular challenge to be an easy task, however. These sorts of ultra-rare treasures can only be handed in at certain spots, so it’s fair to assume that you may have to run a daunting oceanic gauntlet to cash in your prize… Our Onus Is On Bonuses It’s not all precarious pillaging with Pop-Up Plunder – there’s plenty on offer for those of you who just want to enjoy the good vibes of a Community Weekend and score some free stuff. As is customary, the glorious Season Nine Community Weekend flag, in regal tones of purple and gold, will magic its way into the flag boxes of all logging in over the event period. Season Nine Community Weekend also very good news if you’re a fan of fondling foliage, as the Touch Grass Emote will be available for all those logging in during the event period too! While we don’t actually want you to touch any grass during the event – ideally, we want you playing Sea of Thieves – we figured it’d come in handy for those moments of heightened emotion, where you feel like it could be quite refreshing to remind yourself of your roots.

And where else to show off your new emote, than in front of a stunning Community Weekend-themed picture wall? Off the back of the popularity of our Anniversary Community Weekend picture wall, we’re bringing back event picture walls to the Sea of Thieves! Except but this time we’re adding them at every Outpost, so you won’t need to trek to Port Merrick to snap your perfect selfie. The new style of wall will also have a flag that represents the Community Emissary Grade at that given time too, so you’ll know how at a glance how Grade progression is going. We’ve even managed to squeeze in a functional grog barrel to the scene, so pirates won’t have to travel far for a celebratory beverage. We’ll cheers to that! For those of you wanting to very literally fly the Season Nine Community Weekend flag at home, you’re in luck – as we’ve got a beautiful time-limited flag and t-shirt heading to the Rare merchandise store for the duration of the weekend. And if any of you folks are looking for an excuse to pick up some additional pirate goodies at a bargain price, the discount code SOTCOMMUNITYWEEKEND will knock off 30% of the cost to an array of Sea of Thieves swag in that very same store, so it’s worth browsing their digital aisles now to see if there are cute items you’d like to earmark for the occasion. Even though Twitter will no longer power our Community Emissary Grade, we’ll still have a big focus on the #SeaOfThievesCommunityWeekend hashtag for fun stories and stunning screenshots, artwork and gameplay clips – so please make sure you spend some time sharing the good vibes on all the usual social media channels. Our Community team will be captaining the channels all throughout the weekend, ready to retweet our favourite posts! Our Community events have always served as a super opportunity to spotlight some of our community’s MVPs. So, if you think either you or one of your crewmates deserves fame and a particularly fetching hat, look no further as #BeMorePirate returns in a supercharged fashion for Community Weekend. It’s our favourite opportunity to celebrate our amazing artists, players, creators and community good eggs – so as is customary, we’ll be scouring our channels and the #BeMorePirate hashtag for amazing Sea of Thieves stories, from now until the end of the event. Worthy winners will be awarded the Hat of Unfeasibly Glorious Fame, so get your submissions in soon to see if your antics warrant your pirate’s bonce being crowned with such a special bonnet. Glove In The First Degree Fashion-focused freebooters, you’re also in luck – it’s time to return to the catwalk for a bonus round of #StyleOfThieves! With just one category to dress for, we’re calling your whole crew into the spotlight this time, with the theme Crew Couture. Show off your cohesive looks or represent your roles on the ship with a screenshot, and you and up to three crewmates could each win a set of Fashionista’s Finery Gloves! Submissions will open 10am UTC October 1st and run for just 24 hours, with the four winning crews being revealed by October 6th. Post your screenshot during the submission window using #StyleOfThieves and the theme title on Twitter, Instagram, and our Official Discord server via the Pirate Fashion Channel, or reply to the #StyleOfThieves Facebook post to enter. Herd Community The most important thing you need to do to prepare for the Season Nine Community Day is contact your crew – or, if you’re a lone wolf, mark some time in your diary for a serious bit of solo Sloopin’. Check your Reputation tabs, your Commendations and our Outpost stocks to see what sorts of targets you can set your sights on, and plot a course for adventure alongside all our other wonderful pirates in this celebration of Sea of Thieves’ super community! We can’t wait to see you on the seas.

@Partners @Partner Legends It has come to our attention there seems misinformation spreading around the community regarding Safer Seas. After some investigation into the source of this misinformation, we've traced at least some of the initial seeds of this back to creator videos. We want to clarify here: we have not made any comment about how servers will be set up for Safer Seas, nor have we discussed how they will work - so seeing comments around how they will absolutely be 'client side' or 'peer-to-peer' is causing confusion from our players. It's also confusing us, because this is incorrect. Please be conscious that the information you put out there influences our players and can cause a lot of unnecessary conversation and swirl about particular hot topics. Our community looks up to you all as trusted creators who are more in-the-know than most, which is how we've seen an incorrect assumption understood by many as the truth - which is causing a headache for us internally. Because we do not comment on infrastructure externally, it's made even harder for us to correct this false information - because to do so, we'd then have to discuss internal systems. If you have any questions about how Safer Seas servers might work, please ask us. It's likely we can answer your question, but depending on the answer we may ask you not to share this outside of the Creator server - but nevertheless, we're only ever a ping away and would be much happier answering what we can than having to firefight a particularly frustrating and incorrect narrative born out of a misunderstanding.

Hey @Affiliate @Partners @Partner Legends - Following on from this post https://discord.com/channels/550315686636027908/625611888956276736/1166036304811790417 i wanted to drop a quick update. The reduction in Ship and Player limits have had a positive impact in reducing the severity of the performance issues, however we can still see many servers under strain and adversely affected. The team have a strong lead now on both the root cause of the Server Performance issues and the Cannon Firing bug, we'll be getting fixes into testing asap and then expedite into Live as soon as we can in advance of the next scheduled update. Once we have a clear release plan defined we'll share this information wider, but for now i wanted to fire a quick heads up as i know you are all dealing with the discourse in your communities. Appreciate also that there are open topics awaiting a response in other areas of balancing and fixes, but right now we are focusing on stabilising the experience and will then share updates on the other topics. Hey @Affiliate @Partners @Partner Legends - Dropping a further quick update here. Today a server hotfix began to roll out in Live which delivered two improvements. The first was a fix to a vulnerability that allowed bad actors to affect the stability of a server and cause others to be disconnected, and the second was a fix to the now dubbed 'Cannon Sploot'... (Players failing to fire from Cannons). This will rollout over time as new servers spin up, but has been making it's way out during the day. The team are now preparing a further client hotfix for early next week to address further issues we've trapped and fixed. This will include Server Performance improvements, Skull of Destiny Voyage returning to the shipwright with price rollback, Guild Distinction Emissary Flag fix along with some smaller bug fixes. We'll assess how this update lands in Live before we commit to any changes RE Ship and Player limit and whether further hotfixes may be necessary as we approach November's update. On the balancing side, the team are in the weeds assessing the balancing of Combat and Guild progression right now, so while i don't have further updates to share the team have the feedback and are assessing where we may need to make changes. As before please don't share this update verbatim, but rather use the knowledge of the actions were taking behind the scenes, hopefully gives you all confidence that the team are working hard to get us back to a stable state. Appreciate your patience and support as we're working through these areas - I'm confident we can bring some stability back to the experience here soon!

Hey @Partners , @Partner Legends , @Affiliate We have now completely moved the application process for Partner and Variety Programmes to Lurkit and updated the information on our website. What changes for existing Partners and Variety creators: Nothing much, all you need to do is apply to our Creator Programme there (if you haven't already) and DM me, so I can get you in right away - https://www.lurkit.com/company/sea-of-thieves-creator-programme This will ensure you are included in our list of affiliated creators and are able to participate in any special Lurkit-exclusive activities we are planning in the future. This Discord Server will remain our main platform for communication, latest updates, feedback, collabs and many other things. Lurkit is meant to streamline our application process, reach out to new creators and run campaigns rewarding SoT content creation on top of all existing perks. What it means for new creators who would like to join: They will no longer have to fill in the old form and will have to create or log in to their Lurkit account to apply instead. Great news is that Lurkit does the majority of tedious but necessary checks for us automatically, which improves application processing time from our side. We won't have to dig for hidden gems through hundreds of (mostly random or unrelated) submitted forms anymore, and creators will find out about the decision on their applications much quicker. What about those who applied just before we closed the old form? There are about two hundred submissions in our backlog awaiting the decision at the moment. Their applications are safely stored in our system and we'll prioritise reviewing their channels first in the next couple of weeks. Other Lurkit things worth highlighting: We have a game key giveaway campaign active and running, so if you know a creator who maybe wanted to give SoT a try, but was stopped by a price tag - feel free to send them this link - https://www.lurkit.com/campaigns/1b99-sea-of-thieves . We review and usually approve the keys in bulk 2-3 times a week. Lurkit has plenty of tools to run missions, quests and custom campaigns to engage and reward creators and we are excited to use them all for you, Creator Crew and general marketing activities in the future. We are cooking something already, just need to figure out the reward system and schedule. If you run into any issues while using Lurkit: Please reach out to their support on support@lurkit.com or use their knowledge base to find quick answers to the most common questions https://support.lurkit.com/ . As always, feel free to dm me if you have any questions. (edited)

Hey @Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate Jumping in here to share a little update on the ellusive Mimic Dart you've no doubt seen teased at the Outposts recently. The Mimic dart will allow players to temporarily hide in plain sight and confuse their opponents by taking on the appearance of another player or skeleton in the world. As the team moved through production on Season 14 this part of the Blowpipe's arsenal was deprioritised so we could build confidence in other areas. We see so much value in the stories this tool could create that instead of descoping it from the Season, we instead made the call to delay its launch. The Mimic Dart will therefore not be available at Season 14's launch but will be arriving later in the season, with our target being our December update. Appreciate there has been lots of speculation on this feature since it began being teased at the Outpost and hadn't yet arrived in Insiders for testing. We will communicate this on the launch of Season 14 in the Release Notes, but you are free to use your channels to communicate that; The Mimic Dart is still being worked on The team made the call to prioritise hardening other areas of the Seasons feature set for launch Will not be available for Season 14 Launch and will arrive later in the Season Appreciate you've all been holding your tongues on this one, so feel free to share this news with your communities when asked and confirm that we'll share more on this feature soon. Any questions, feel free to shout me in #ask-rare-2-electric-boogaloo

Hey @Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate - It's me, back again! As we've been closing down the build for launch on Thursday we've seen enough signals that the Hanging from Ledges feature is not currently stable enough to ship right now. We are therefore temporarily disabling Hanging From Ledges for launch and will enable it as part of a post-launch hotfix once we've addressed known stability issues and regained shipping confidence. As a result however this does mean that a number of Commendations, Achievements/Trophies and Rewards will be unobtainable at launch. We'll be communicating this as part of the Season 14 Patch Notes going live tomorrow, and there'll be a supporting KBA on the Support Site that gets amplified by our channels. We're sharing this with you all in advance so you are aware and prepared, however consider this information NDA Confidential until we share the news ourselves. What i want to share also here though is that while it's always painful for us to have to delay a feature especially with it's knock on impact to other areas, i champion the teams call here to focus on the player experience at launch and to put our hands up and hold on something that just isn't ready. Reviewing the signals from Internal testing and Insiders reports, this feature set has the indicators to generate instability and bugs to the wider experience and I'm happy to make a pragmatic proactive decision to hold here rather than a reactive apology later down the line. Appreciate your support here come launch tomorrow, reinforcing this approach and reminding players we'll be working hard to get this part of the feature set in players hands as soon as possible. Again, any questions feel free to shout me in #ask-rare-2-electric-boogaloo

Hey @Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate - They say news always comes in three's... The irony of my last update isn't yet lost on me, however today we find ourselves in a similar situation however this time with Traps. While we were on a path to shipping Traps with a known issue today we've seen the impact area of this problem open up and reach a level that will impact regular play, particularly in player to player encounters on ships. I know this is now the second of our Season features to come under the quality spotlight, however we've made the difficult call to temporarily disable Traps alongside Hanging from Ledges from Season 14 launch today. We have similar impact here with a number of Commendations, Achievements/Trophies and Rewards being unobtainable at launch. We will launch with this communicated as part of the Season 14 Patch Notes going live, and supporting KBA on the Support Site. I'm also going to get a proactive State of Play update to the forums to explain the issues and the decisions we've taken here to better inform our players on why we've made these tough calls. Again - sharing this with you all in advance so you are aware and prepared, however consider this information NDA Confidential again until we share the news ourselves. Appreciate your support as always and i know you'll be equally disappointed at losing some value from the season at launch but understand the reasons we've made this decision. Any questions feel free to shout me in #ask-rare-2-electric-boogaloo

@Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate Ahoy dear scallywags! As we head into the winter season, we wanted to give the year a proper send off and something unique for everyone here to get into the holiday spirit. You’ve likely got at least some idea of the Festival of Giving but today we’re happy to announce two additional initiatives which will run concurrently from the 6th – 22nd of December, starting and finishing at 10am UTC. @everyone Important PSA for all creators. It’s been flagged to me that some creators recent use of VODs for re-runs when taking an extended break or otherwise not live for a long period. After raising this with Twitch we’ve been advised that this goes against the site’s ToS and should be avoided. Hey all @Partners , @Partner Legends , @Affiliate We've teased and talked a bit about it few months ago, and now it's actually happening - Twitch Drops are getting a major overhaul! This new system will get implemented with the very next Everyone's Drops event to support Season 16 launch on 23rd-30th May . We will be moving away from running a couple of recoloured sets for many months in a row towards the new system with gold, renown boost, new exclusive titles, voyages and a wide variety of cosmetics. The first four Drops will require 30 minutes of watching per item, the second four will require an hour to unlock each. The rewards will appear in this order and take 6 hours of total watch time to complete: 30 min - 75k Gold 30 min - Renown boost (+1 progress lvl) 30 min - Title 30 min - Emote 1 hour - Voyage 1 hour - Legacy Twitch Drop Cosmetic 1 hour - New Unique Cosmetic 1 hour - Emporium Cosmetic While most things on this list are pretty self-explanatory it's worth mentioning a few important details: We are still cooking up some new Titles and Emotes, so we are going to drop an extra legacy Twitch Drop cosmetic instead of a title and re-run a few of the older emotes for couple of months. New Titles are meant to be fun and unique to the Twitch Drops system and stand out from what you'd normally get in the game upon reaching milestones. These will be mainly based on memes, ridiculous word play and funny SoT community references. Renown boost will take you up one level no matter where you are on the Seasonal Pass (whether you bought Plunder Pass or not). Legacy Twitch Drops Cosmetics will, obviously, use some of the older existing sets and we'll try to pick cosmetics that were not used in a very long time to fill this spot. Unique Cosmetics won't be tied to a full clothing/ship/weapons set anymore. These will either stand completely on their own or represent part of a mini collection of just a few cosmetics. For example, we'll be starting with three items of clothing in a distinct "Twitch Purple" colour. And from there on, it's going to be something different almost every time. There will be an item from the Emporium with a real monetary value in every Drops event. We could look up and provide the value in case you find that information useful. And finally, the promo images for new Drops system are also going through some major design changes to accommodate twice as many goodies, so please expect the new assets as well. Hope you and your viewers will enjoy the new Drops and please let us know if you have any questions! (edited)

Hey all, following up with a long post of useful information from today's call: THE ORB AWAKENS ​ Event Timing ​ - Friday 7 p.m. UTC to Sunday 4 p.m. UTC​ What to Expect​ • No spoilers… but expect familiar Orb energy and cryptic visions​ • The Orb will offer glimpses into the Sea of Thieves world and its future​ • This year, the Orb’s visions may call for more than simply watching, so be ready to follow where its guidance leads.​ Creator Involvement​ • We will continue our tradition of Orb raids with legacy drops across the weekend​ • To be considered, sign up on Lurkit with your gamertag, stream name, and planned stream times​ - https://www.lurkit.com/missions/the-orb-stand-ready-prepare-your-ship-light-your-lanterns • While we cannot guarantee every creator will be included, signups help us understand who wants to be involved HOLIDAY HAULS & LEGENDS OF GIVING In-game Live Event part of the Festival of Giving umbrella. Players will get the ability to share gifts (cosmetics) directly with other players by using special “gift-giving” emotes. By sharing gifts players work together to complete a community goal which unlocks a final reward for everyone playing during the event.​ This event breaks down to two components:​ Holiday Hauls ( @Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate @Variety ) Participating creators receive a unique “Holiday Hauls Emote”, Generous Gift Sails & Banners. ​With these sails they can give a selection of cosmetics – Obsidian Grapple Gun (new), Gilded Phoenix Crest, Obsidian 6 Pack​ Legends of Giving (All Pirate Legends)​ Main in-game Live Event extending the Holiday Hauls mechanic to all Pirate Legends for wide reach.​ Legends receive the “Legends of Giving Emote”, and Generous Gift sails to posturize their intentions and are able to give away a single item – Obsidian Grapple Gun (new)​ The Emote cosmetics & Generous Gift Sails & Banners are granted temporarily ; they will be removed from players once the Event ends on January 2nd .​ TREASURES FOR HOPE – CREATOR SUPPORT AND REWARDS​ Radiant Comet Reward Bundle​ As a thank you, all creators who take part in Treasures for Hope can opt in to receive the Radiant Comet Reward Bundle which includes:​ • Radiant Comet Lantern​ • Radiant Comet Collector’s Sails​ • Radiant Comet Collector’s Figurehead​ • 25,000 Gold​ • 550 Ancient Coins​ How to Claim​ • Sign up at www.treasuresforhope.com​ • Once you receive your welcome email, reply with your Gamertag​ • We will then apply the bundle directly to your account​ For Partners and AA​ If you want to take part and encourage your communities to support the charity, you can request additional giveaway items. We will help you rally your viewers around the campaign.​ If you take part in or are raided by the Orb over the weekend, any shout out for Treasures for Hope would be greatly appreciated (but not required). People are often in a giving mindset during the Orb streams, so any visibility you provide will make a real difference. Top donor rewards The top two fundraising creators with a guest will receive VIP tickets to the Global Gift Gala Marbella Presidential Experience. You and a companion will be visiting the Mediterranean city of Marbella, Spain, to attend The Global Gift Gala as Special Guests, one of Europe’s most anticipated charity events. Aaand that's pretty much all you need to know about events during a very busy festive December this year. I will follow up with another announcement about Twitch Drops and new Giveaway items tomorrow. Hope you're all enjoying the New Season! (edited)

@Partners @Partner Legends Hey hey! Posting more important bits from yesterday's call: TWITCH DROPS 2026 STRUCTURE Here is what you can expect next year starting from next Season launch: Act 1 - Everyone Drops Act 2 - Partner Drops Act 3 - Partner Drops Why We Are Making This Change​ • Partners drive the overwhelming majority of our Twitch performance​ • Returning to a recognisable format reinforces clarity for the community and stops confusion about when and where items can be earned​ • Season Launch visibility gives all creators a moment to share the excitement, while Acts Two and Three provide clear, meaningful exclusivity for Partners​ • This helps ensure the Partner Program remains aspirational and encourages creators to grow toward that level​ What This Supports​ • Stronger, more consistent traffic directed toward trusted Partners who reliably represent our game and deliver high quality content​ • A stable, predictable Drops calendar creators can plan around for the full year​ PARTNER GIVEAWAYS​ In 2026, giveaways will operate differently. Each Season, you will log into Lurkit and claim your giveaway codes through the mission system there. This uses the Universal Codes system, so capturing gamertags won’t be necessary anymore, allowing you to distribute codes directly during your stream. The codes will remain valid for the entire Season, which lasts three months.​ REGULAR MONTHLY GIVEAWAY ITEMS FOR 2026:​ x3 Gilded Phoenix Blowpipe, Grapple Gun, DB Pistol and Throwing Knife sets (replaces GP Ship set from Feb 2026)​ x3 New Partner Programme Sails (available from March 2026, replaces Serpent's Lie sails)​ x3 Gilded Phoenix Ship Crest​s x5 (per season) Plunder Pass​es And extra cosmetics will be available for you to request on top of your regular pool of items for special events as usual:​ ​ GP ship set and GP weapons and more of the items above​ Obsidian 6packs, capstans, a wheel on occasion Ruby Splashtail ship set (reserved for charity events only) (edited)

@Partners @Affiliate @Partner Legends Hi all, I wanted to swing by to say we’ve clocked the suggestion of custom server sessions being span up to quickly share the coveted Legends of Giving items you all have the power to give away. Please see this event as a wonderful opportunity to have some emergent, delightful fun with fellow pirates – despite the pressure you might have from a few folks to give them over in the most boring, transactional way possible. SpecialEffect are a UK based charity who help people with severe physical disabilities to access videogames. By using solutions ranging from modified controllers to eye-tracking sensors, SpecialEffect find ways for people to play to the very best of their abilities. https://www.specialeffect.org.uk/ GameBlast is the UK’s biggest annual charity gaming marathon weekend in aid of SpecialEffect, taking place all throughout February, ending in a big celebration weekend between Friday 27th Feb and Sunday 1st March! Your supporters can donate and choose in-game rewards (available NOW!) and as a special fundraiser bonus, if your page raises £100 or more, you'll get a super special edition pin badge directly from the charity! Sign-ups for the general public will be open next week, but you're not the general public, YOU'RE SPECIAL, so you get to sign up early and help us spread the word about this event and the wonderful cause it supports JOIN HERE: https://tiltify.com/+rare-ltd/sotgb26 Click on "Support this campaign" to create your own fundraising page. If you have any questions, please ask! You can tag me in one of the channels here, or my DMs are always open

p.s. there's even more fun stuff planned for GameBlast 2026, i'll keep you posted as soon as i'm able to announce it, but lemme just say, i'm EXCITED!! As some of you may know, this charity is close to my heart, i've seen the incredible impact they make first-hand and i'm so proud and happy to be able to support their work with events like this, so i just wanna say a personal thank you in advance to anyone who signs up for this one

Hey @Partners and @Partner Legends , ❗ IMPORTANT ANNOUNCEMENT ABOUT PARTNER GIVEAWAYS ❗ Starting from 20th February we will be switching to Lurkit Mission system to distribute the codes for your regular giveaways. Each Season, you will log into Lurkit and claim your giveaway codes through the mission system there. The missions will use the Universal Codes system, so capturing gamertags won’t be necessary anymore, allowing you to send codes directly to your winners. The codes will remain valid for the entire Season, which typically lasts three months. How the new system will work: There will be one mission per type of reward (GP sets, Plunder Passes, etc). All missions will be set to "automatic review" so you should get your codes pretty much immediately after accepting them. You will get the amount of codes to last you until the end of the Season in one batch. Why are we making this change? 1) Less bureaucracy for everyone involved - you won't need to collect and submit gamertags weekly, and we won't have to manually apply the rewards and hope for the best 2) Less waiting time for your winners - they get the codes they are free to redeem at their own leisure 3) No more need for you or us to deal with wrong gamertags. As long as your winner is logged into correct account on SoT website - they'll get their rewards 4) Reduce our reliance on Sharepoint that is well known for it's access shenanigans (you'll still have to use it for assets and other reasons, though) Universal Codes: The codes you'll be getting are universal codes that can only be redeemed on https://www.seaofthieves.com/redeem/redeem-your-code . These codes will apply rewards account-wide, no matter which platform player uses to play SoT - PC, Xbox or Playstation. These codes will also be time-limited and redeemable before the end of the Season. What you need to do now: Nothing IF you are registered on Lurkit and part of our Creator Programme there already. The missions will just appear for you to apply for and claim the keys. If not please make sure that: you have a verified account on Lurkit apply to our Creator Programme Sea of Thieves https://www.lurkit.com/company/page/sea-of-thieves-creator-programme dm me to accept you asap The Rewards: Here is a list of Codes that will be available for you to claim from 20th February to 19th March (Season 19 launch): 3 Gilded Phoenix Ship sets 3 Gilded Phoenix Crests 3 Serpent's Lie Sails 5 Plunder Passes In the near future, New Gilded Phoenix weapon set will replace the current Gilded Phoenix ship set and new Partner Programme Sails will also be introduced, but I'll bring you more details on that later! What about extras for special occasions? A few extra cosmetics will be available for you to request on top of your regular pool of items for special events as usual: GP ship set and GP weapons and more of the items above Obsidian 6packs, capstans, a wheel on occasion Ruby Splashtail ship set (reserved for charity events only) And finally... Naturally, many of you who run regular events and giveaways will end up with a little more rewards to giveaway until the end of this Season than you are supposed to due to this Sharepoint/Lurkit eras overlap. Treat it as a little bonus from us for making you switch to a completely new, hopefully, a much more streamlined process. After this switchover period and starting from Season 19 everyone will have the same number of codes to use per Season with all other additional cosmetics available per request here - https://discordapp.com/channels/550315686636027908/1277932653806620683 Thank you for making it to the end of this announcement. Hope the new system will help you run your giveaways much easier in the future! Will appreciate any feedback or questions you may have (edited)
